div.head: The Minister in China (Schurman) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
220. Your 82, April 8, 6 p.m.Not printed; it informed Minister of substance of telegram no. 104, Apr. 8, to the Ambassador in Great Britain, p. 892. British Minister telegraphing his Government that he and I agree that proposal that the Chinese Eastern
Railway bear the cost of Technical Board is undesirable on its own merits on the following grounds:

Loss of independence and prestige; already excessive administrative budget of the railway and possible encouragement to the existing agitation against foreign participation in the management. British Minister adding that I hope that question of withdrawal of British representative may be held over pending further discussion between the United States and Great Britain.
